questions.question_text: Which duty requires an Idaho agent to follow their client's lawful instructions?
questions.explanation: Obedience requires the agent to follow the client's lawful instructions. Loyalty means putting the client's interests first, disclosure means sharing relevant information, and accounting means tracking funds.

questions.question_text: A seller's agent learns of a competing offer. Which duty requires them to inform the seller?
questions.explanation: The duty of disclosure requires agents to share all relevant information with their client that could affect their decisions. A competing offer is clearly material information the seller needs to know.

questions.question_text: An agent knows their client is getting divorced and may be willing to accept a low offer. Can the agent share this with the buyer?
questions.correct_answer: No, this is protected by the duty of confidentiality
distractors: Yes, buyers have a right to know motivation
Yes, if it would speed up the transaction
Only if the seller gives verbal consent
questions.explanation: The duty of confidentiality protects the client's personal motivations and circumstances. The seller's divorce and potential willingness to accept less is confidential and cannot be shared without written permission.

questions.question_text: Which set of duties does an Idaho agent owe to a represented client?
questions.correct_answer: Loyalty, obedience, disclosure, confidentiality, care, accounting
distractors: Honesty, good faith, and competent service only
questions.explanation: Idaho agents owe the full set of fiduciary duties to clients: loyalty, obedience, disclosure, confidentiality, reasonable care, and accounting. The shorter list (honesty, good faith, competent service) applies to all parties.

questions.question_text: A buyer's agent discovers a property has structural issues not mentioned in the listing. What duty requires disclosure to the buyer?
questions.explanation: The duty of disclosure requires the agent to inform their client of all material facts, including property defects they discover. Structural issues are clearly material to a buyer's purchase decision.

questions.question_text: What duties does an Idaho licensee owe to ALL parties in a transaction, including customers?
questions.correct_answer: Honesty, good faith, and disclosure of material adverse facts
distractors: The same full fiduciary duties as clients
questions.explanation: All parties (including non-represented customers) are owed honesty, good faith, disclosure of material adverse facts, and competent service. The full fiduciary duties are reserved for represented clients.

questions.question_text: A licensee working as a nonagent discovers a major plumbing defect during a showing. Must they tell the buyer?
questions.correct_answer: Yes, material adverse facts must be disclosed to all parties
distractors: No, nonagents have no disclosure obligations
Only if the buyer asks about plumbing
Only if the defect was previously disclosed by the seller
questions.explanation: Even as a nonagent, the licensee must disclose material adverse facts to all parties. This duty applies regardless of agency status — it protects consumers from hidden defects.

questions.question_text: What distinguishes the duties owed to a 'client' versus a 'customer' in Idaho?
questions.correct_answer: Clients receive fiduciary duties; customers get honesty and competence
distractors: There is no difference in duties
Customers receive loyalty; clients do not
Clients only get disclosure; customers get full duties
questions.explanation: Clients (represented parties) receive the full set of fiduciary duties: loyalty, obedience, disclosure, confidentiality, care, and accounting. Customers (non-represented parties) are owed honesty, good faith, disclosure of material adverse facts, and competent service.

questions.question_text: An agent representing a seller learns the property has a history of flooding. Must this be shared with a prospective buyer who is not a client?
questions.correct_answer: Yes, material adverse facts must be disclosed to all parties
distractors: No, only the seller's agent's client needs to know
Only if the buyer has a home inspection
Only if the flooding occurred in the last year
questions.explanation: Material adverse facts about a property must be disclosed to ALL parties, including non-client buyers. A flooding history is a material adverse fact that affects the property's value and desirability.

questions.question_text: Which duty owed to all parties requires the licensee to provide knowledgeable assistance?
questions.explanation: Competent service is owed to all parties and requires the licensee to provide knowledgeable, skilled assistance. Loyalty, obedience, and accounting are fiduciary duties owed only to clients.

questions.question_text: After an agency relationship ends, which duty continues to apply?
questions.explanation: Confidentiality continues after termination of the agency relationship. The former agent must not disclose the client's confidential information even after the relationship has ended.

questions.question_text: A seller unilaterally revokes their agency agreement with a broker before the listing period ends. What may result?
questions.correct_answer: The seller may have liability for early termination
distractors: The broker can refuse to terminate
questions.explanation: Revocation by the principal terminates the agency but may create liability. If the listing agreement specifies terms for early termination, the seller may owe compensation or damages to the broker.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is NOT a valid method of terminating an agency relationship in Idaho?
questions.explanation: Filing an IREC complaint does not terminate an agency relationship. Valid methods include: expiration, completion, mutual consent, revocation, renunciation, death/incapacity, and bankruptcy.

questions.question_text: A licensee fails to provide timely agency disclosure. What consequence may follow?
distractors: A fine from the county court
questions.explanation: Failure to provide timely disclosure is a violation of Idaho real estate law subject to disciplinary action by IREC. The transaction is not automatically voided, but the licensee faces Commission sanctions.

questions.question_text: An agent's former client asks them to keep a secret about a property defect in a future transaction with a new agent. Should the former agent comply?
questions.correct_answer: No, material defect disclosure is required regardless
distractors: Yes, the former client's wishes take priority
Only if the new agent doesn't ask about defects
questions.explanation: While confidentiality survives termination for personal information (motivations, finances), it does not override the duty to disclose material adverse facts about a property. Property defects must be disclosed to protect all parties.
